scaleCorrectionNote: Round 3 rescale from 1-7 (hook framing) to 1-5 (docs/scoring-rubric.md authoritative). Scores re-derived from rubric definitions, not mechanical divide.

hypotheses.name: Clean-slate v1.0 rebuild
hypotheses.statement: SemVer-major bump of @palantirKC/claude-schemas (0.2.1 -> 1.0.0) plus full palantir-mini v1.0 restructure plus NEW ~/ontology/ shared-core namespace plus new /palantir-dispatch + /ontology-register skills plus legacy /ship + /orchestrate rewrite, shipped as one atomic 3-consumer migration wave, yields the canonical Palantir 4-pillar mirror faster and cleaner than incremental patches; the breaking change is explicitly sanctioned by NEXT_SESSION_ONTOLOGY_REBUILD.md.
hypotheses.claim: A single SemVer-major release that lands schemas v1.0 + plugin v1.0 + home ontology/ + new skills together is the only change scope that structurally repairs ForwardProp (WMO-031 healthStatus=broken) because the home-repo ontology/ layer cannot be added additively without also forcing palantir-math and mathcrew to switch their extension point; once you must touch all three, you pay all breaking-change cost anyway, so amortize the v1.0 bump in the same wave.
hypotheses.dlaJustification: DATA: adds 4 missing primitives (Struct, ValueType, SharedPropertyType, AIPLogicFunction) to primitives/ layer and promotes home /ontology/ as the shared-core Module. LOGIC: fixes ForwardProp pipeline (WMO-031) by inserting the home layer between schemas and per-project ontology. ACTION: adds AutomationDeclaration + WebhookDeclaration + PalantirDispatchSkill + OntologyRegisterSkill (4 primitive-missing entries in dlaMapping.A). SECURITY: adds CapabilityToken (L2) + MarkingDeclaration (L2/3). LEARN: adds ScenarioSandbox + expands EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY 10 -> 16+.

supportingEvidence.note: 6-layer orchestration map requires Builder + Semantic Core + Digital Twin + Governance + LEARN + Integration — v0.2 covers ~3; v1.0 closes the gap structurally, not incrementally.
Home repo missing ~/ontology/ layer — the ONLY structural fix is to introduce it, which forces consumer extension-point change (breaking).
User explicitly permits: schemas v1.0 major bump + plugin v1.0 restructure + namespace reorganization + legacy deletion.
Comment explicitly states Deploy + Merge phases 'deferred to v1' — v0 behavior encoded for replacement, not extension.
6/27 hook events covered — 21-event gap cannot be closed via OCP-only additions without also renaming internal registration contracts.
Version string mismatch (package.json 0.1.0 vs CLAUDE.md 0.2.0) — v1.0 reconciliation is the clean fix vs preserving ambiguity.

contradictingEvidence.note: Current 3 consumers all pinned at v0.2.1 — SemVer break forces atomic migration; any slip causes supply-chain fracture.
Universalization already shipped additive minor bumps W1-W4; v1.0 over-rides recent investment rather than composing with it.

testCriteria: pm-verify 6-phase gates all PASS in each consumer after migration PR merges
bunx tsc --noEmit passes across all 3 consumers after peerDep bump
events.jsonl contains ontology_registered event for each of 9 newly-promoted primitives
ForwardProp healthStatus transitions broken -> healthy per world-model post-migration re-normalization
3-consumer atomic migration ships within 1 release cycle (no stale pin for >48h)
architectureImplications: Home repo gains ontology/ directory — new authority layer between schemas and per-project
palantir-math + mathcrew extension points switch from direct schema import to home-ontology re-export
managed-settings.d gains L2/L3 deny rules enforcing CapabilityToken + Marking
/palantir-dispatch becomes the canonical skill invocation surface — legacy slash-commands become dispatched intents
Codegen gains deterministic header contract enforced by pm-verify phase 4 (post-write drift)
dc5PrinciplesInvoked: DDD: bounded contexts per domain (schemas axes, home core, per-project extensions, plugin subsystems)
OCP: v1.0 SemVer-break restricted to legacy retirement and layer insertion; new primitives remain additive
DRY: ~/ontology/ is THE DRY win — eliminates palantir-math x mathcrew ConceptPrimitive duplication
PECS: Reducer<In,Out> + Edits[] + intent types preserved as producer-extends/consumer-super boundaries
hypotheses.forwardPropImpact: Repaired. WMO-031 healthStatus broken -> healthy. Home ~/ontology/ added as missing shared-core layer between schemas and per-project ontology, closing the authority chain.
hypotheses.backwardPropImpact: Automated path enabled. New schema_refinement + drift_detected event types emitted by evaluator signals auto-feed /ontology-register. WMO-032 healthStatus partial -> healthy.
hypotheses.fourPillarFidelity: High. Each of ontology/aip/foundry/maven pillars gets an explicit v1.0 target per fourPillarMap: schemas v1.0 canonical (ontology), /palantir-dispatch + /ontology-register + rewritten /ship + /orchestrate (aip), plugin v1.0 with 10+ MCP tools + 15+ hooks + 6/6 validation phases + L1/L2/L3 RBAC (foundry), atomic 3-consumer peerDep migration (maven).

hypotheses.name: Incremental v0.3 evolution
hypotheses.statement: Additive minor bumps on @palantirKC/claude-schemas (0.2.1 -> 0.3.x -> 0.4.x -> ... -> 0.9.x), palantir-mini plugin 0.2 -> 0.3 adding missing primitives (Struct, ValueType, CapabilityToken) while preserving legacy types.ts + semantics.ts layout, home ~/ontology/ added behind a feature-flag without breaking existing consumers, skill rewrites deferred, each project upgrades peerDep independently, yields a safer migration path that preserves the supply-chain stability earned by the W1-W4 universalization waves.
hypotheses.claim: SemVer minor-range (0.3.x) releases compose cleanly with the current peerDependencies pin at 0.2.x (three-project consumers simply bump caret to 0.3.x when ready). No atomic migration wave is required. The same 9 missing primitives can be delivered as a series of additive releases (0.3 adds DATA primitives, 0.4 adds SECURITY, 0.5 adds ACTION, etc). Breaking changes only happen when explicitly justified by evidence — not pre-emptively bundled.
hypotheses.dlaJustification: DATA: additive — 3 new primitives (Struct, ValueType, SharedPropertyType) shipped in 0.3.x with existing types.ts interfaces aliased for one release cycle then deprecated. LOGIC: ForwardProp partially repaired — ~/ontology/ directory added but legacy types.ts remains as fallback, so per-project consumers may opt-in layer by layer. ACTION: incremental — /palantir-dispatch + /ontology-register deferred; existing /ship + /orchestrate continue unchanged. SECURITY: CapabilityToken added in 0.4.x without removing existing L1 RBAC. LEARN: ScenarioSandbox + EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion ship in 0.5.x — no breaking change required.

supportingEvidence.note: v0.2.0 -> v0.2.1 (2026-04-17) shipped additive exports map + root index.ts + README — additive pattern already proven.
W1-W4 universalization shipped across 3 consumers via additive peerDep strategy; recent investment compounded by continuing pattern.
Current L1 fragment is forward-compatible — L2 (CapabilityToken) can be layered additively without touching deny rules.
Existing policy already gates on peerDep pin compatibility — enforces smooth minor-bump migration.

contradictingEvidence.note: Home-repo ~/ontology/ cannot be 'added additively' — the moment it exists, palantir-math and mathcrew MUST choose to extend it or not. Any 'feature-flag' approach just defers the consumer decision without repairing ForwardProp.
Phases 5-6 Deploy + Merge explicitly 'deferred to v1' — source-level marker indicates rewrite was planned, not incremental extension.
Pre-existing version mismatch (package.json 0.1.0 vs docs 0.2.0) accumulates drift over incremental releases unless reconciled at a SemVer-major boundary.
21-event gap requires substantial hook registration refactor that has high coupling cost in a series of minor bumps.

testCriteria: pm-verify 4-phase gates continue to PASS on 0.3.x and each subsequent minor bump
bunx tsc --noEmit succeeds in each consumer across the 0.3 -> 0.9 range without lockstep upgrade
events.jsonl backfilled with primitives_added events per incremental release
ForwardProp healthStatus stays partial throughout — only reaches healthy when per-project consumers opt in
No session cleanup required between bumps (incremental does not force state reset)
architectureImplications: Home repo ontology/ added but extension is opt-in — consumers can stay at direct-schema import
Legacy types.ts + semantics.ts preserved as shims for duration of 0.x range
Event log accumulates version drift markers (each bump emits a version_bumped event)
Skill rewrites deferred — /ship + /orchestrate remain 465 LOC + 406 LOC user-scope
Codegen continues to emit AUTO-GENERATED marker without deterministic header contract until a future bump
dc5PrinciplesInvoked: OCP: primary win — every change is an additive extension, no modification of existing types
DRY: partially applied — home ontology enables DRY for opt-in consumers but legacy duplication persists until deprecation cycle completes
DDD: preserved at axis level but home/per-project boundary remains blurred until opt-in complete
hypotheses.forwardPropImpact: Partially repaired. Home ~/ontology/ added but consumer extension remains optional; legacy types.ts + semantics.ts persist as fallback. WMO-031 healthStatus stays broken -> partial until last opt-in lands.
hypotheses.backwardPropImpact: Manual. Evaluator R-gate signals remain manual Phase-B planning; no new event types that auto-feed refinement. WMO-032 healthStatus remains partial across the 0.x series.
hypotheses.fourPillarFidelity: Medium. Pillars get incremental improvement but preserve legacy: ontology axis adds 3 DATA primitives in 0.3 and skips 6 others until later; aip pillar unchanged (no dispatcher); foundry pillar adds 1-2 MCP tools per bump (5 -> 6 -> 7 ...); maven axis benefits from continued additive discipline but delays v1.0 canonical moment indefinitely.

scenarios.description: Standard execution: 4-week rebuild in 3 Phase-B waves. Wave B1 (week 1-2): schemas v1.0 tag with 9 missing primitives + home ~/ontology/ scaffold + plugin v1.0 mcp-server expansion 5 -> 10 tools + hooks 6 -> 15 + validation 4 -> 6 phases + L1/L2/L3 RBAC. Wave B2 (week 3): 3-consumer atomic peerDep migration PR bundle. Wave B3 (week 4): /palantir-dispatch + /ontology-register ship; /ship + /orchestrate rewritten to consume events.jsonl; pm-verify 6/6 gates enforce codegen header contract + schema lock.

assumptions.statement: 4-week sprint is enough
3-consumer atomic migration window coordinates within 48h
9 missing primitives delivered with TypeScript sketches only (no deep business logic)
pm-verify phases 5-6 (Deploy + Merge) implemented per INT-021 comment intent
Codegen header contract adopted without breaking existing src/generated/ consumers

contradictions.statement: H-B argues incremental preserves supply-chain stability
contradictions.resolution: Atomic 3-consumer migration window (<=48h) is acceptable precisely because all 3 consumers are in one org (palantirkc) and can coordinate. H-B supply-chain argument applies to cross-org distribution, not intra-org coordinated releases.

evaluationScores.rationale: All 5 assumptions backed by tier-1/2 internal evidence (INT-001/004/011/013/016/019/020/021/023/026 Official + INT-027 Synthesis); per rubric §1 score 4 — most backed, minor gap on A2 external benchmarks for atomic migration window.

Moderate per rubric §2 score 3 (2-4 weeks custom work). Large surface but bounded: 9 primitive additions + plugin 5 -> 10 MCP tools + 21-event hook expansion + skill rewrite + 3-consumer atomic migration.
Moderate per rubric §3 score 3. Atomic migration delay causes noticeable impact but mitigation available (coordinated revert). Worst case 48h stall, no data loss, no compliance violation.

Partially reversible per rubric §4 score 3. SemVer-major bump requires significant refactoring to reverse; v1.0.1 rollback tag available within days; architectural commitment to home ontology layer persists.
2-4 week MVP timeline per rubric §5 score 3. Full v1.0 at week 4.
Fully compliant per rubric §6 score 5. pm-verify 6/6 phases + L2/L3 RBAC + CapabilityToken enforced. Exceeds current L1-only state.
Stable per rubric §7 score 4. Bun 1.x + Claude Code v2.1.110 GA + OSDK 2.0 patterns (INT-014) 2-5 years active maintenance; palantir-mini + schemas 1+ year production.
All 5 domains (D/L/A/S/LEARN) cleanly classified per dlaMapping. Transition zones (EditFunction L vs A) explicitly handled via SH-03 heuristic. Per rubric §8 score 5.

Full chain: research -> schemas -> home -> project -> contracts -> runtime per world-model.forwardProp.authorityChain. Home ~/ontology/ layer inserted; runtime names match ontology. Per rubric §9 score 5.

Most operations agent-composable per rubric §10 score 4: /palantir-dispatch intent classifier + /ontology-register registration + events.jsonl lineage substrate + plugin MCP tools agent-addressable. Minor gap: agent prompt updates for 9 new primitives.
Provisional per rubric §11 score 3 (moderate uncertainty pre-T7/T8).

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase II Operational -> Phase III AI-First bridge
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Targets the Phase 1 -> 2 transition per decision-log.devConPhaseClassification. Schemas v1.0 + plugin v1.0 = operational substrate. Skills as dispatchers + lineage-driven PR = Phase III AI-First reach prototype.

risks.statement: 3-consumer atomic migration window slips beyond 48h
risks.mitigation: Pre-stage migration PRs on feature branches; coordinate merge via Lead dispatcher; rollback plan via v1.0.1 shim tag

risks.statement: 9 new primitives ship as TypeScript sketches without deep business logic validation
risks.mitigation: pm-verify phase 3 runtime + T9 eval suites catch incomplete primitives; v1.0.x patch releases close gaps incrementally

risks.statement: Legacy types.ts + semantics.ts retirement breaks undocumented consumers
risks.mitigation: Schema lock rule + grep across 3 consumers before retirement; keep-read-only-until-retire verdict per legacyAudit.schemasFiles preserves rollback option

whatWouldChangeDecision: Evidence that 3-consumer atomic migration coordination requires > 1 week (would re-favor H-B staged bumps)
Discovery of a fourth in-scope project that was not accounted for in atomic migration window
Breaking-change manifest at v1.0 cut-over exceeds 20 symbols (would indicate legacy-retirement scope underestimated)

scenarios.description: Optimistic: 3-week execution. Wave B1 compresses to 10 days via parallel branch work (schemas-v1 + plugin-v1 + home-ontology-scaffold as 3 concurrent PRs). Wave B2 atomic migration completes within 24h (all 3 consumers have blueprint-ready peerDep updates pre-staged). Wave B3 skills ship in week 3. Fresh pm-verify 6/6 gates PASS first try. No rollback. Lead-direct orchestration succeeds without team-lead debate.

assumptions.statement: Parallel branch work compresses Wave B1 to 10 days
3 consumers have pre-staged migration PRs before Wave B2 starts
pm-verify 6/6 gates pass on first v1.0 tag
All 9 new primitives have complete implementations, not sketches

contradictions.statement: Best-case assumes parallel branch work is coordination-free; reality has inter-branch conflicts
contradictions.resolution: Pre-registered merge order (schemas -> plugin -> home) eliminates most conflicts since each depends on prior; remaining conflicts in plugin vs schemas resolved via 24h coordination window

evaluationScores.rationale: Tier-1/2 on most; A2 (pre-staged consumer PRs) depends on unmeasured consumer readiness. Per rubric §1 score 4 (minor gaps).
Straightforward per rubric §2 score 4 (1-2 weeks proven pattern) under best-case parallelism; 3-week total with clear dependencies.
Minor per rubric §3 score 4 (small delay or workaround needed). Best-case assumes no rollback, clean gates.
Same architectural commitment as S-A1 base. Per rubric §4 score 3.
1-2 weeks working demo (Wave B1 at 10 days); 3 weeks full v1.0. Per rubric §5 score 4.
Full 6/6 pm-verify gates + L2/L3 RBAC in same wave. Per rubric §6 score 5.
Same stack as base. Per rubric §7 score 4.
All 5 domains classified; no degradation. Per rubric §8 score 5.
Fully repaired; no difference from base. Per rubric §9 score 5.
Actions, functions, queries all composable — agent prompts updated within v1.0 release (vs base which defers to v1.0.1). Full Phase III reach. Per rubric §10 score 5.
Provisional pre-T7/T8.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase II Operational -> Phase III AI-First full reach
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Best-case pushes into Phase III AI-First proper: /palantir-dispatch as semantic dispatcher + BackwardProp automation goes live.

risks.statement: Parallel branch conflicts require > 24h coordination
risks.mitigation: Fallback to base-case 4-week plan if parallelism stalls

risks.statement: Pre-staged consumer PRs stale if upstream changes land mid-Wave-B1
risks.mitigation: Consumer PR rebases automated via github actions

whatWouldChangeDecision: Consumer readiness audit reveals any of 3 cannot pre-stage within 5 days
Inter-branch conflict rate exceeds 20% on Wave B1 dry-run

scenarios.description: Pessimistic: 8-week execution with 2 rollback cycles. Wave B1 schemas v1.0 tag fails pm-verify phase 3 runtime on first attempt due to incomplete AIPLogicFunction implementation. Rollback to v1.0-rc1; rework 1 week. Wave B2 atomic migration stalls because palantir-math deferred items (F-1 full curve helpers) conflict with primitive renames — 2-week recovery with mathcrew waiting. Wave B3 ships /palantir-dispatch but /ontology-register hit by L2 CapabilityToken not being ready. Legacy /ship + /orchestrate not rewritten within window — pushed to v1.1.

assumptions.statement: pm-verify phase 3 runtime gate fails on first v1.0-rc
palantir-math F-1 deferred items (jsxGraph parametric-curve body extraction) conflict with shared primitive renames
L2 CapabilityToken implementation under-scoped for Wave B3 /ontology-register
Skill rewrites (/ship + /orchestrate) exceed 4-week scope and defer to v1.1

contradictions.statement: Worst-case assumes rollback but repo evidence shows no prior v1.0 rollbacks to pattern against
contradictions.resolution: Flagged as NextExperiment: prototype schemas v1.0-rc1 in isolated worktree before production tag to measure rollback readiness

evaluationScores.rationale: Core assumptions supported; A2 + A4 inferential — per rubric §1 score 3 (core supported, some inference).
Difficult per rubric §2 score 2: rollback loops + inter-project coordination stalls + skill rewrite deferral = 1-2 month timeline.
Severe per rubric §3 score 2: 8-week slip + deferred skill rewrite + L2 CapabilityToken gap = significant rework; still recoverable.
Difficult to reverse per rubric §4 score 2: rollback already happened once; architectural commitment to home ontology persists.
8 weeks = 1-2 months significant lead time per rubric §5 score 2.
Partially compliant per rubric §6 score 3: L1 RBAC remains, L2 deferred to v1.0.x patch — gap present but not blocking.
Same stack; variance is in execution, not stack. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Most concepts clear; some primitives (MarkingDeclaration, ScenarioSandbox) shipping in v1.1 leave transition-zone items flagged. Per rubric §8 score 4.
Chain mostly complete; minor gaps in S + LEARN propagation until v1.0.x patches. Per rubric §9 score 4.
Core operations composable (dispatcher ships); complex workflows (/ontology-register) require human orchestration due to delay. Per rubric §10 score 3.
Provisional pre-T7/T8.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase II Operational (Phase III deferred to v1.1)
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Worst-case achieves Phase 2 but Phase 3 AI-First slips to v1.1.

risks.statement: Cascading rollbacks erode team confidence in v1.0 strategy
risks.mitigation: Pre-publish explicit rollback runbook; treat first failure as learning not blocker

risks.statement: palantir-math F-1 deferred items block shared primitive rename
risks.mitigation: Resolve F-1 full (parametric-curve body extraction to curveHelpers.ts) BEFORE Wave B2

risks.statement: Skill rewrite deferred to v1.1 means legacy 865 LOC stays production-critical 4+ weeks longer
risks.mitigation: Ship /palantir-dispatch with legacy skill fallback path

whatWouldChangeDecision: If worst-case timeline (8 weeks) materializes, reassess H-B incremental merit after Wave B1 rollback
L2 CapabilityToken scoping audit reveals readiness < 60% going into Wave B3

scenarios.description: What breaks v1.0? (1) Hostile merge-window slip: palantir-math PR lands while mathcrew CI is red; atomic migration bisects — consumers end up on mixed v0.2.1 and v1.0.0 for 5 days. (2) Codegen drift: pm-codegen regenerates src/generated/* with v1.0 header but consumer's local build uses cached v0.2 generated — runtime object RID mismatches leak into events.jsonl. (3) Schema-lock bypass attempt: contributor edits types.ts directly (deny rule stale in per-project managed-settings.d/ fragment) — silent drift. (4) /palantir-dispatch mis-routes intents after EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion — events emitted under wrong variant, replay_lineage returns incomplete lineage.

assumptions.statement: Atomic migration can bisect if a consumer CI fails mid-window
pm-codegen cache invalidation gap produces generated file drift
Per-project managed-settings.d/ fragment may lack updated schema-lock deny rule
Intent classifier error rate > 5% under expanded EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY

contradictions.statement: Adversarial A3 contradicts managed-settings.d deny rule enforcement claim in INT-023
contradictions.resolution: Per-project fragment drift is exactly the scenario L2 CapabilityToken on 'schema-write' would catch. Confirms worst-case need for L2 in v1.0.

evaluationScores.rationale: A1-A4 each supported by one tier-1 source but adversarial composite is inferential. Per rubric §1 score 3.
Mitigating 4 adversarial paths adds pm-verify gates + merge-window lock + L2 enforcement = novel patterns, 1-2 months. Per rubric §2 score 2.
Severe per rubric §3 score 2: bisected consumer state + codegen drift + silent schema-lock bypass compound to user-facing failure.
Partially reversible per rubric §4 score 3: bisect recoverable via CI rollback; codegen drift via force-regenerate; schema-lock needs per-project fragment audit.
Adversarial mitigation extends timeline to 4-6 weeks. Per rubric §5 score 3.
L2 CapabilityToken mitigates schema-write bypass; pm-verify 6/6 catches codegen drift; governance mostly compliant minor gaps. Per rubric §6 score 4.
Stack mature; exposure is process + tooling integration. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Classifications preserved; adversarial paths target enforcement gaps not classification. Per rubric §8 score 4.
Backend propagation healthy but frontend/consumer-side temporarily divergent during migration window on 1/3 consumers. Per rubric §9 score 3.
Core composable; /palantir-dispatch mis-routing degrades intent classification; post-ship calibration required. Per rubric §10 score 3.
Provisional — T7/T8 should explicitly test codegen drift + intent mis-route paths.

scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Adversarial scenario targets enforcement layer integrity at Phase 1 -> 2 boundary.

risks.statement: Bisected consumer state during atomic migration window
risks.mitigation: Pre-publish merge-window lock protocol; require all 3 consumer CIs green before Wave B2 merge authorization

risks.statement: Codegen cache invalidation gap
risks.mitigation: Force-regenerate step mandatory in pm-verify phase 4 post-write; emit codegen_regenerated event with schema hash

risks.statement: Schema-lock deny rule drift across consumer managed-settings.d fragments
risks.mitigation: Plugin bundles authoritative fragment; consumer fragments merge, not override

risks.statement: /palantir-dispatch mis-routing after EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion
risks.mitigation: T9 eval suite includes intent-classification adversarial test set; mis-route event type emitted for retrospective

whatWouldChangeDecision: Adversarial evaluation run reveals mitigation cost > 1 week on any single path (consider splitting v1.0 into v1.0 + v1.1 to defer highest-cost mitigation)
Intent classifier mis-route rate exceeds 5% in T9 prototype eval

scenarios.description: Standard execution: 6-week rollout across 0.3.x -> 0.6.x. 0.3.x (week 1-2): Struct + ValueType + SharedPropertyType primitives shipped; types.ts interface aliases preserved. 0.4.x (week 3): CapabilityToken added in policies/ with L1 RBAC opt-in; no L2 enforcement yet. 0.5.x (week 4): AutomationDeclaration + WebhookDeclaration primitives. 0.6.x (week 5-6): home ~/ontology/ directory added with opt-in flag; palantir-math + mathcrew each decides independently whether to extend. Skill rewrites deferred to v1.0 'when needed'. Plugin MCP tool count grows 5 -> 7 (adds ontology_schema_get + codegen_trigger). Hook count grows 6 -> 10. Validation stays 4-phase.

assumptions.statement: 6-week timeline accommodates 4 minor bumps
types.ts interface aliases preserve existing consumer code unchanged
Consumers opt in to home ontology on their own timelines
Skill rewrites genuinely deferrable — /ship + /orchestrate continue to work
Each minor bump is independently reversible via pin revert

contradictions.statement: Assumption A3 (consumer opt-in to home ontology) contradicts WMO-031 ForwardProp repair requirement — if even 1 consumer stays at direct-schema import, ForwardProp stays broken
contradictions.resolution: Acknowledged: H-B accepts 'partial' ForwardProp repair as the tradeoff for preserving supply-chain stability. This is the central irreconcilable tradeoff between H-A and H-B.

evaluationScores.rationale: Assumptions backed by tier-1/2 internal evidence; additive pattern proven in W1-W4 waves. Per rubric §1 score 4.
Straightforward per rubric §2 score 4: each minor bump is 1-2 weeks of additive work; no atomic coordination.
Minor per rubric §3 score 4: no SemVer break; per-consumer pin revert handles failure. Small delay or workaround needed on version drift.
Mostly reversible per rubric §4 score 4: each minor bump independently reversible via peerDep pin revert + caret-range constraint; days of work per rollback.
First value (Struct + ValueType) at 2 weeks; full 0.3-0.6 suite in 6 weeks. Per rubric §5 score 3 (MVP timeline).
Partially compliant per rubric §6 score 3: L1 RBAC remains; L2 opt-in without enforcement for duration of 0.x.
Same stack as H-A. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Core D/L/A clean; Security (L2 opt-in) and Learn (ScenarioSandbox deferred) thin. Per rubric §8 score 3.
Significant gaps per rubric §9 score 2: home ontology added but opt-in. Runtime names diverge from ontology on consumers that stay at direct schema import.
Limited composability per rubric §10 score 2: no /palantir-dispatch, no /ontology-register; agents need custom integration per ontology registration operation.
Provisional pre-T7/T8.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I Golden Tables -> Phase II Operational (slow bridge)
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Incremental path remains at Phase 1 + partial Phase 2 for duration of 0.x series; Phase 3 unreachable without dispatcher + /ontology-register.

risks.statement: Version drift accumulates — consumers end up spread across 0.3/0.4/0.5/0.6 pins simultaneously
risks.mitigation: pm-verify schema-version gate enforces minimum 0.3.x pin once any consumer upgrades

risks.statement: Home ontology opt-in stalls — 1 consumer never extends, ForwardProp stays broken indefinitely
risks.mitigation: Accepted tradeoff per hypothesis contradictionStatus; /ship adds warning when consumer not on home-ontology extension

risks.statement: Legacy types.ts + semantics.ts stay production through 0.x range; future v1.0 still needs the same retirement work
risks.mitigation: Mark types.ts for retire in 0.9 deprecation notice; document delta

whatWouldChangeDecision: Evidence that 1+ consumer is unwilling to opt in to home ontology within 3 minor bumps (forces back to H-A atomic)
Version drift audit shows > 2 minor-version spread across 3 consumers for > 1 week (supply-chain stability claim fails)

scenarios.description: Optimistic: 4-week rollout. All 3 consumers eagerly opt in to home ontology by 0.4.x. Each minor bump ships in 1 week clean with pm-verify 4/4 PASS. Legacy types.ts interface aliases retired opportunistically in 0.6.x without breaking anything. Plugin v0.3 ships parallel to schemas 0.3.x with 3 new MCP tools. /palantir-dispatch prototyped as experimental behind feature flag by 0.6.x. By end of 0.9.x (6 weeks out), effective v1.0 state reached without breaking-change ceremony.

assumptions.statement: All 3 consumers opt in to home ontology by 0.4.x
Legacy aliases retire opportunistically without consumer coordination
Plugin v0.3 + schemas 0.3.x ship in parallel without conflict
/palantir-dispatch shippable experimentally without full intent-registry

contradictions.statement: Best-case A2 (opportunistic alias retirement without coordination) conflicts with schema-lock discipline
contradictions.resolution: Each alias retirement happens in the bump that replaces it; consumer pin bump to the next minor forces adoption. No separate coordination needed because the aliases' only purpose was alias.

evaluationScores.rationale: A1 (all 3 consumers eager) is the optimistic linchpin — evidence present but partial support. Per rubric §1 score 3.
Straightforward per rubric §2 score 4 via parallel plugin/schemas releases.
Negligible per rubric §3 score 5: no break, eager consumer adoption, fast pm-verify passes.
Same per-bump reversibility as S-B1; mostly reversible. Per rubric §4 score 4.
Working demo at 1 week; effective v1.0 at 4-6 weeks. Per rubric §5 score 4.
L2 CapabilityToken ships with enforcement — mostly compliant, minor gaps. Per rubric §6 score 4.
Same. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Most primitives classified; MarkingDeclaration deferred to 0.9.x — minor transition-zone item flagged. Per rubric §8 score 4.
Best-case repair: all 3 consumers on home ontology by 0.4.x = chain mostly complete; minor naming convention differences remain across bumps. Per rubric §9 score 4.
Core composable; experimental /palantir-dispatch still needs calibration; complex workflows require human orchestration. Per rubric §10 score 3.
Provisional pre-T7/T8.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase II Operational (approaching Phase III via experimental dispatch)
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Best-case B achieves near-parity with H-A base via eager consumer adoption.

risks.statement: Optimistic assumption about consumer eagerness does not materialize
risks.mitigation: Fallback to S-B1 base plan — still delivers value, just slower ForwardProp repair

risks.statement: Experimental /palantir-dispatch ships without full intent registry; unstable for 2+ releases
risks.mitigation: Feature-flag + eval calibration per release

whatWouldChangeDecision: If S-B2 best-case materializes, re-evaluate v1.0 label value — effective v1.0 may arrive without ceremony

scenarios.description: Pessimistic: 12-week drift across 6 minor bumps (0.3.x through 0.8.x) without reaching v1.0-equivalent state. Consumers drift into mixed-pin state: home at 0.5.x, palantir-math at 0.3.x, mathcrew at 0.4.x. Each pin bump requires individual migration PR + review. Home ontology added in 0.6.x but only home + mathcrew extend; palantir-math stays at direct schemas import for 6+ weeks. Skill rewrites (/ship + /orchestrate) never happen. EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion partial — lineage replay returns fragments for some sessions. L2 CapabilityToken added but no consumer enforces. v1.0 ceremony never reached; incremental strategy becomes 'permanent 0.x'.

assumptions.statement: Consumer pin drift accumulates over 6+ bumps
palantir-math holds back on home ontology extension due to F-1 deferred items
Skill rewrite deferral becomes permanent
No forcing function pulls strategy to v1.0 ceremony
L2 CapabilityToken opt-in stays at 0% after 3 bumps

contradictions.statement: A4 (no forcing function) has no evidence ID
contradictions.resolution: Flagged as NextExperiment: survey historical 0.x -> 1.0 transitions in peer projects to measure drift-to-ceremony likelihood; accepted as partial-evidence pending research

evaluationScores.rationale: A4 has no evidence; A1 + A5 inferential — significant assumptions unsupported. Per rubric §1 score 2.
Each bump individually straightforward but cumulative drift management adds coordination overhead — moderate per rubric §2 score 3.
Moderate per rubric §3 score 3: no break at any single point but cumulative drift means 1/3 consumer permanently divergent; noticeable impact, mitigation available.
Mostly reversible per rubric §4 score 4: each bump reversible; overall strategy reversible back to H-A at any point.
12 weeks without reaching v1.0 equivalent state per rubric §5 score 1 (>2 months long development cycle).
Significant gaps per rubric §6 score 2: L2 added but not enforced for entire 0.x range; major compliance work needed to reach v1.0 target governance.
Stack unchanged. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Core D/L/A classified; Security (L2 enforcement 0%) and Learn (lineage fragments) thin. Per rubric §8 score 3.
No meaningful propagation per rubric §9 score 1: 1/3 consumer never extends home ontology; ForwardProp chain has structural break indefinitely.
No agent composability per rubric §10 score 1: no dispatcher, no /ontology-register, incomplete MCP tool coverage — manual orchestration required for all operations.
Provisional pre-T7/T8.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I Golden Tables + partial Phase II (stuck bridge)
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Worst-case H-B never reaches Phase II fully; Phase III unreachable.

risks.statement: Pin drift becomes permanent architectural state
risks.mitigation: Forcing function: after N minor bumps (e.g. 6), require all consumers to pin same minor or flag in pm-verify

risks.statement: v1.0 ceremony never scheduled; strategic coherence lost
risks.mitigation: Time-box 0.x range to 8 weeks; if drift persists, auto-escalate to H-A atomic

risks.statement: Skill rewrite permanently deferred
risks.mitigation: Every 3rd minor bump includes a skill-rewrite workstream allocation

whatWouldChangeDecision: If worst-case H-B drift exceeds 8 weeks, triggers automatic fall-back to H-A atomic per R-B3-02 mitigation
Consumer pin spread > 2 minor versions persists > 1 week — reassess hypothesis entirely

scenarios.description: What breaks H-B? (1) Schemas axis-version-gate confusion: 0.3.x bumps ontology axis 1.12 -> 1.13 but rendering axis stays 0.1.0; consumer CI validates against wrong axis matrix. (2) Types.ts interface-alias silent ambiguity: consumer imports from types.ts (legacy) vs primitives/ (new) without realizing; TypeScript resolves to alias, runtime uses primitive — semantic drift between compile-time and runtime. (3) Home ontology partial-opt-in creates network-of-exceptions: palantir-math at direct schemas import while mathcrew at home ontology means a single shared primitive (ConceptPrimitive) has two different runtime objects. (4) EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion from 10 -> 12 -> 14 -> 16 across bumps: events emitted under one variant, replayed under a later bump find variant missing, return incomplete lineage.

assumptions.statement: Per-axis version drift causes CI validation ambiguity
Types.ts alias preserves compile-time but not runtime identity
Partial opt-in produces divergent shared-primitive runtime objects
Incremental EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion breaks lineage replay across versions

contradictions.statement: Adversarial A3 contradicts H-B central claim that opt-in preserves stability — instead it introduces hidden correctness bug
contradictions.resolution: Exposes H-B core weakness: the 'preservation of stability' applies to compile-time but not runtime semantics. This is a genuine cost of H-B that narrows the supply-chain stability argument.

evaluationScores.rationale: Each adversarial path supported by 1 tier-1 source; composite inferential. Per rubric §1 score 3.
Mitigation requires per-axis version matrix + runtime identity checks + lineage replay backfill = moderate 2-4 weeks. Per rubric §2 score 3.

Critical per rubric §3 score 1: runtime-vs-compile-time semantic drift + divergent shared-primitive runtime objects are silent correctness bugs — compliance-adjacent safety issue (events.jsonl lineage loses integrity).
Partially reversible per rubric §4 score 3: each bump still reversible but accumulated bugs harder to trace back to specific bump.
Adversarial mitigation extends timeline 1-2 months beyond base. Per rubric §5 score 2.
L1 RBAC preserved; correctness bugs are governance-adjacent. Per rubric §6 score 3 (partially compliant).
Same stack. Per rubric §7 score 4.
Adversarial paths target enforcement gaps that map to classification confidence — core clean, S + LEARN thin. Per rubric §8 score 3.
Per rubric §9 score 1: ontology disconnected from runtime on consumers that stay at direct schema import; divergent runtime objects mean no meaningful propagation for shared primitives.
Per rubric §10 score 1: no dispatcher + lineage replay returning fragments = agents cannot reliably compose ontology actions; manual execution required.
Provisional — T7/T8 should stress-test incremental EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY replay.

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I Golden Tables + partial Phase II (adversarial stress)
scenarios.implementationPhaseRationale: Adversarial H-B reveals Phase 1 -> 2 bridge has hidden correctness issues that Phase III cannot tolerate.

risks.statement: Per-axis version-gate CI ambiguity
risks.mitigation: Explicit per-axis version matrix published per release; pm-verify gates against matrix

risks.statement: Types.ts alias runtime identity drift
risks.mitigation: Retire aliases within 1 release; require primitives/ import path in codegen output

risks.statement: Divergent shared-primitive runtime objects across consumers
risks.mitigation: Forcing function: once home ontology added, all 3 consumers MUST extend within 1 release (undermines H-B opt-in principle)

risks.statement: EVENT_TYPE_REGISTRY expansion breaks lineage replay
risks.mitigation: Every variant-addition release includes lineage replay backfill; never remove variants

whatWouldChangeDecision: Runtime identity drift test on S-B4 mitigation R-B4-02 failing in T9 eval (triggers H-A fallback)
Divergent shared-primitive objects detected across 2+ consumers (triggers R-B4-03 forcing function that collapses H-B into H-A)

simulationRuns.reason: Lead correction: rescale from 1-7 to 1-5 per docs/scoring-rubric.md authoritative; scores re-derived from rubric definitions

evidenceGapsIdentified: S-A3 A2 palantir-math F-1 conflict is inferential
S-B3 A4 no forcing function has no evidence

changesApplied: Resolved S-A1 C1 contradiction by acknowledging intra-org coordination reduces atomic-migration risk (H-B supply-chain claim scoped to cross-org)
Resolved S-A3 C1 (rollback assumption without prior pattern) by creating NextExperiment for schemas v1.0-rc1 isolated worktree test
Resolved S-A4 C1 (managed-settings.d/ fragment drift) by confirming L2 CapabilityToken is exactly the mitigation — strengthens H-A rationale
Resolved S-B1 C1 (opt-in irreconcilable with ForwardProp repair) as explicit hypothesis-level tradeoff, not scenario bug
Resolved S-B3 C1 (evidence gap on forcing function) by flagging NextExperiment for peer-project 0.x->1.0 transition survey
Resolved S-B4 C1 (opt-in introduces runtime identity bug) as genuine H-B weakness — narrows supply-chain-stability argument
All 8 scenarios scored on 11 dimensions (initial scale 1-7 per Lead framing)
evidenceGapsIdentified: S-B3 A4 forcing function behavior remains inferential — deferred to post-blueprint research

scoringRubric.scale: 1-5 per docs/scoring-rubric.md (authoritative). 5 = best in all dimensions.
scoringRubric.prototypeValidationNote: Pre-T7/T8 all scenarios set to 3 (moderate uncertainty provisional) per rubric §11 and Lead instruction — will be updated after prototype.

weaknesses: Agent Composability 1.75 (no dispatcher; no /ontology-register — agents need custom integration per operation)
ForwardProp Health 2.0 (partial repair — opt-in does not close WMO-031 gap)
Time-to-Value 2.5 (6-12 weeks without reaching v1.0-equivalent state)

aggregate.winRationale: H-A out-scores H-B on the strategic dimensions called out as primary objectives in NEXT_SESSION_ONTOLOGY_REBUILD.md: ForwardProp Health (4.25 vs 2.0), Agent Composability (3.75 vs 1.75), D/L/A Fit (4.5 vs 3.25), Governance Compliance (4.25 vs 3.0), and 4-pillar fidelity (High vs Medium). H-B wins on the pragmatic dimensions (Reversibility 3.75 vs 2.75, Implementation Difficulty 3.5 vs 2.75, Risk Severity 3.25 vs 2.75), but the user directive explicitly accepts those tradeoffs: 'schemas v1.0 major bump allowed', 'palantir-mini v1.0 full restructure allowed', 'namespace reorganization allowed', 'legacy deletion allowed' (decision-log.constraints.breaking-changes severity=high, negotiable=false). Critical structural finding: WMO-031 ForwardProp healthStatus=broken cannot be repaired additively — adding home /ontology/ layer forces consumer extension-point change regardless of SemVer strategy, so H-A atomic approach is the only path that reliably reaches healthy state. H-B partial opt-in explicitly accepts permanent broken ForwardProp as cost of stability, contradicting the rebuild central objective. Total mean 3.50 vs 3.00.
aggregate.criticalTradeoff: ForwardProp repair (strategic) vs migration cost (pragmatic). User directive resolves the tradeoff in favor of strategic repair by explicitly sanctioning breaking changes.
